definitions.definition: These rights are guaranteed by the Constitution to every citizen of India. They are contained in Part III of the Constitution. These rights are justiciable, meaning they can be enforced by the courts.
definitions.example: Right to equality before the law (Article 14)

definitions.definition: These are guidelines for the government to follow while making laws and policies. They are contained in Part IV of the Constitution. These principles are not justiciable, meaning they cannot be enforced by the courts.
This right is guaranteed by Article 14 of the Constitution. It states that all citizens are equal before the law and are entitled to equal protection of the law.
definitions.example: No person shall be denied equality before the law

definitions.definition: This right is guaranteed by Article 19 of the Constitution. It states that all citizens have the right to freedom of speech and expression, assembly, association, movement, and occupation.
This right is guaranteed by Article 21 of the Constitution. It states that no person shall be deprived of his life and liberty except according to procedure established by law.
This right is guaranteed by Article 21A of the Constitution. It states that every child has the right to free and compulsory education.

definitions.term: Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression
definitions.definition: This right is guaranteed by Article 19(1)(a) of the Constitution. It states that all citizens have the right to express their opinions freely through any medium.
This right is guaranteed by Article 16 of the Constitution. It states that all citizens are entitled to equal opportunities in matters of public employment.
This right is guaranteed by Article 19(1)(b) of the Constitution. It states that all citizens have the right to assemble peacefully without arms.
This right is guaranteed by Article 19(1)(c) of the Constitution. It states that all citizens have the right to form associations or unions.
This right is guaranteed by Article 19(1)(d) of the Constitution. It states that all citizens have the right to move freely throughout the territory of India.
This right is guaranteed by Article 19(1)(g) of the Constitution. It states that all citizens have the right to engage in any occupation, trade, or business.
This right is guaranteed by Article 300A of the Constitution. It states that no person shall be deprived of his property except by authority of law.
These are rights that can be enforced by the courts. They are contained in Part III of the Constitution.
definitions.example: Right to equality before the law (Article 14)

definitions.definition: These are rights that cannot be enforced by the courts. They are contained in Part IV of the Constitution.
This is a process by which the Constitution can be amended. It requires a two-thirds majority in both the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha.
These are guidelines for the government to follow while making laws and policies. They are contained in Part IV of the Constitution.
These are duties that every citizen of India is expected to perform. They are contained in Part IVA of the Constitution.
definitions.example: To defend the country and render national service

definitions.definition: This right is guaranteed by Article 32 of the Constitution. It states that every citizen has the right to move the Supreme Court for the enforcement of their fundamental rights.
